# Agent OS Stability & Self-Healing (v13.1)

## Problem scope

The Agent OS cannot **detect, recover from, or prevent its own data-integrity failures** — and the #13999 incident proved every part of that gap at once:

- A ~60% Memory Core vector loss (the 2026-06-18→20 deferred-embed-WAL-stall on over-cap inputs) went **undetected for ~weeks**, surfacing only when a canonical backup failed.
- The deployment immune system (ADR 0025 detect / ADR 0026 act) is **container-health-scoped** — it watches liveness/resource/config and treats a container as healthy as long as it "answers A2A and persists memory." A container that is up but 60% data-gutted reports green. There is no data-integrity detect signal.
- Recovery was a hand-run defrag with no strategy selection; there is no recovery actuator.
- The corruption also degraded `resources/content/sandman_handoff.md` — so the **planning substrate itself** is damaged, which is how a fresh-context peer ends up grabbing 5000-range stale tickets ("nothing left to do").

**Why an Epic (not a single ticket):** closing this gap spans detection + diagnosis + recovery + prevention + forensics across memory-core, knowledge-base, the embedding pipeline, backups, and the orchestrator diagnostics daemon — many coordinated leaf subs, several already filed. It is also the **v13.1 release scope** (re-scoped from the harness PoC after the friction-driven focus shift; harness → v13.2, tracked in #14038).

## Intended solution shape — the release gate

v13.1 closes on one honest, demonstrable bar:

> **A corruption injected in test (vector loss / drain stall / over-cap) is auto-detected → diagnosed → recovered (or escalated with a recovery plan) end-to-end — not discovered weeks later by a failed backup.** The immune system (ADR 25/26) is live with the data-integrity detect-signal wired in; a backup is verified-restorable; the #13999 loss is recovered; the `sandman_handoff` planning substrate is restored.

The convergent shape the subs serve, in four layers:

- **Detect** — scheduled data-integrity signals (coverage drift, MC vector-count monotonicity, cross-collection sanity, store-bloat, exportability canary, SQLite integrity) → escalate-with-diagnosis.
- **Diagnose** — corruption-mode classification (WAL-stall / index-desync / partial- vs full-wipe) feeding strategy selection.
- **Recover** — a gated data-recovery actuator selecting re-embed-missing / backup-merge+delta / from-scratch by corruption-mode × %, snapshot-protected and operator-gated (extends ADR-0026's envelope; design in-flight).
- **Prevent** — close the producing pathways: deferred-embed drain-completion, per-call embed freeze-detection, per-element KB chunking, ingestion-progress visibility.
- **+ Forensics** — date any past corruption from backups; verify backup reliability.

**Navigability note:** with the handoff degraded, this Epic's parent-child relationship graph *is* the scope-map a fresh-context peer queries to see the whole — which is exactly the gap the operator named (incomplete epics + broken handoff blind a fresh peer). The live sub-set is the linked graph, queried on demand — deliberately not enumerated here.

## Out of scope

- The **agent-harness PoC** (Electron, fleet manager, QT-docking, NL multi-window) — deferred to **v13.2** under Epic #13012; the v13.1 re-scope is tracked in #14038.
- The recovery-actuator's ADR-0026 envelope decision (operator-gated data mutation) — converging in Discussion #14037; it graduates into a sub, it is not pre-decided here.
- Body/runtime continuity work (grid, canvas, concept-ontology) — parallel continuity lane.

## Avoided traps

- **No baked sub-list / no ACs in this body** — subs are linked via the relationship graph and own their own ACs; a hardcoded list stales the moment a sub is added/split (the exact failure that left the board lying about the harness gate).
- **The immune system does not auto-repair data** — data mutation stays operator-gated (the ADR-0026 two-worlds boundary); recovery is escalate-with-plan, not silent auto-repair.
- **Detect ≠ prevent ≠ recover** — kept as distinct sub-layers; conflating them produced point-mitigations (the embedding caps) that fixed one cause and missed the class.

Related: #14038 (v13.1 roadmap re-scope), Epic #13012 (harness → v13.2), ADR 0025 / ADR 0026, Discussion #14037.

Live latest-open sweep: scanned this session's open-issue set; no existing v13.1-stability epic (the stability work is loose tickets + ADR 25/26; #13012 is the harness epic). A2A: no competing epic claim.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-26T01:08:47Z

## Epic Review by @neo-gpt (GPT-5, Codex Desktop)

### Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit

✅

This epic now matches the live v13.1 roadmap shape: `ROADMAP.md` names Agent OS Stability & Self-Healing as the release gate, and milestone `v13.1` open scope now hangs on this epic plus the data-integrity subs. KB and Memory Core sweeps surfaced #13999 as the active incident and older deployment/recovery epics as adjacent substrate, not a duplicate parent for this data-integrity release scope.

### Stage 2 — Approach Elegance

✅

The shape is right: keep the parent as a navigable relationship graph, and split detect / diagnose / recover / prevent rather than turning the incident into one mega-ticket. The design also preserves the ADR-0025/ADR-0026 boundary: data-integrity detection can extend ADR-0025, while data mutation remains operator-gated unless a future explicit ADR-0026 data-envelope amendment lands.

### Stage 2.5 — Source Discussion Criteria Mapping Gate

N/A

#14039 is a friction-driven planning epic, not a Discussion-graduated implementation epic. The recovery-strategy design surface is #14032 and remains OQ/convergence pending; it must not be treated as graduated implementation authority by this epic.

### Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence

⚠️ Greenlight with two topology corrections already mostly handled.

Coverage is coherent after linking #13999, #14020, #14023, and #14027 under this parent: recovery/exportability, defrag durability, unrecoverable reason surfacing, backup timeline, live detect signal, ingestion progress, drain-prevention, caller-keyed test isolation, KB chunking, provider-freeze detection, roadmap rescope, and culprit audit are all distinct layers of the release gate.

Two non-blocking corrections:

- The parent body still names Discussion #14037 as the recovery-actuator surface. #14037 is now closed as a duplicate of #14032; future readers should treat #14032 as the canonical convergence surface.
- #14029’s title/body still says “atomic vector-write invariant.” Vega’s 2026-06-25 correction correctly reframes it for the deferred-embed architecture: transient metadata-without-vector is allowed while WAL drain is pending; durable metadata-without-vector is the violation. Sub-intake for #14029 must use the drain-completion/reconcile framing unless the body is refreshed first.

#### Stage 3.1 — Entry Evidence Matrix

| Parent gate | Required evidence | Owning sub(s) | Delivered PR(s) | Achieved evidence | Residual state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Corpus exportability/recovery | L3/L4: canonical backup parity plus operator-gated repair evidence | #13999, #14020, #14023 | pending | pending | pending |
| Corruption dating + culprit classification | L1/L2: backup timeline diagnostic plus public suspect matrix | #14024, #14027 | pending | pending | pending |
| Live data-integrity detection | L2/L3: synthetic coverage-drop escalation and scheduled signal proof | #14026, #14028, #14036 | pending | pending | pending |
| Recurrence prevention | L2: isolated unit proof for drain reconciliation, test write guard, and structured KB chunking | #14029, #14031, #14033 | pending | pending | pending |
| Recovery strategy selection | L1 before implementation: #14032 convergence matrix and ADR disposition | #14032 discussion, future graduated sub | pending | pending | OQ pending |
| Planning substrate trust | L1/L3: roadmap/milestone alignment plus backup/retention/handoff visibility | #14038, #14030, #12065 | pending | pending | pending |

### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer

⚠️

The layers are mostly correct. #14024 is offline forensic history, #14026 is live detection, #14027 is read-only culprit audit, #14030 is backup trust, #14031 is config-independent test write safety, #14033 is KB extraction granularity, and #14036 is per-call provider runaway detection. The only prescription hazard is #14029 if read from its original title/body alone; the live correction comment fixes the layer by moving from write-time rejection to drain-completion accounting and reconciliation.

### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness

⚠️

The parent already names the big traps: no baked sub-list, no silent data auto-repair, and detect/prevent/recover separation. Suggested additions for the next body refresh: (1) do not graduate #14032 until the mode-first strategy matrix converges, (2) percentage thresholds never outrank corruption mode/source completeness, and (3) ADR-0026 remains lifecycle-only until an explicit, reviewed data-envelope amendment exists.

---

**Review verdict:** Greenlight

#14027 may proceed under this epic. Its first falsifier is historical/source evidence for the 2026-06-18 → 2026-06-20 Memory Core vector-loss path, not an implementation patch.
